achievements.label: Cities Conquered
achievements.subtext: Damascus, Homs, Aleppo, Jerusalem, Al-Hira & more

timeLine.title: The Path of the Unconquered
timeLine.description: Tracing the strategic masterclasses of history’s greatest undefeated general, from the deserts of Arabia to the gateways of empires.

battles.name: Battle of Uhud
battles.tacticalNote: Only defeat inflicted on Muhammad. Khalid used flanking maneuver through Wadi Qanat.
battles.significance: Proved Khalid's tactical genius before his conversion

battles.name: Battle of Mu'tah
battles.tacticalNote: Broke 9 swords. Coordinated safe withdrawal of Muslim troops.
battles.significance: Earned title 'Sword of Allah' from Prophet Muhammad

battles.name: Battle of Buzakha
battles.tacticalNote: Tayy tribe defected before battle. Tulayha fled to Syria.

battles.name: Battle of Yamama
battles.tacticalNote: Four assaults. Final storming of enclosed 'Garden of Death'.

battles.name: Battle of Walaja
battles.tacticalNote: Double envelopment – hid cavalry behind hills
battles.significance: First major Persian army destroyed in open field

battles.name: Siege of Al-Hira
battles.tacticalNote: City surrendered. Churches and palaces protected.
battles.significance: First tribute from Iraq: 60,000–90,000 dirhams annually

battles.name: Battle of Ajnadayn
battles.tacticalNote: First major confrontation with Byzantines in Syria

battles.name: Siege of Damascus
battles.tacticalNote: Khalid's forces scaled walls and breached Bab Sharqi while others negotiated
battles.significance: First major Syrian city to fall

battles.name: Battle of Yarmouk
battles.tacticalNote: Split cavalry into two wings. Used camel hobbles as defensive barriers. Enveloped Byzantine left flank.
battles.significance: Greatest victory. 50,000+ Byzantine casualties. Destroyed last Byzantine army in Syria. Opened Levant to Muslim rule.

battles.name: Siege of Homs
battles.tacticalNote: Quarter of Church of St. John reserved for Muslim use
battles.significance: First Syrian city with large Muslim population

battles.name: Conquest of Qinnasrin
battles.tacticalNote: Routed Byzantine force under Minas. Spared inhabitants who claimed Arab descent.
battles.significance: Caused Heraclius to abandon Syria for Constantinople

strategicAchievements.title: Tactical Masterpieces
strategicAchievements.description: Khalid's innovations redefined military science, proving that speed and audacity could dismantle empires.

testimonials.text: ...then the flag was taken by one of Allah's Swords (Khalid bin Al-Walid) and Allah gave them victory.
testimonials.speaker: Prophet Muhammad
testimonials.context: After the Battle of Mu'tah, 629 CE — referring to Khalid ibn al-Walid

achievements.name: The Desert March
achievements.description: Marched 500–800 men across 6 days and 5 nights of waterless desert from Iraq to Syria.
achievements.innovation: Drank water stored in camels' stomachs. Had 20 camels drink excessively, sealed their mouths, slaughtered them daily for water.
achievements.outcome: Arrived in Syria to command the conquest. Described by historian Moshe Gil as 'a feat which has no parallel.'

achievements.name: Double Envelopment at Walaja
achievements.description: First recorded use of double envelopment in Islamic military history.
achievements.innovation: Hid cavalry behind hills to flank the Persian army from both sides.
achievements.outcome: First major Sasanian army destroyed in open field.

achievements.name: Camel Hobble Defense at Yarmouk
achievements.description: Used camels as mobile defensive barriers.
achievements.innovation: Hobbled camels in rows to create barriers Byzantine cavalry could not penetrate. Archers fired from behind.
achievements.outcome: Byzantine cavalry momentum halted, flanks exposed.

achievements.name: The Siege of Damascus
achievements.description: A complex multi-gate siege against a major fortified metropolis.
achievements.innovation: Simultaneous coordination of specialized units across all gates, coupled with an elite 'suicide squad' to scale the walls under cover of a feast night.
achievements.outcome: Captured the city with minimal casualties, turning it into a logistical hub for the Levant.

items.quote: “Truly, he is a Sword of Allah that He has unsheathed against the disbelievers.”
items.author: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)

items.quote: “Women are incapable of giving birth to the likes of Khalid.”
items.author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

items.quote: “Farewell, Syria... What an excellent land this is for the enemy!”
items.author: Heraclius

footer.rights: All Rights Reserved. Designed for the legacy of the Sword of Allah.

seo.title: Khalid ibn al-Walid | The Undefeated Commander
seo.description: Explore the strategic brilliance and military legacy of Khalid ibn al-Walid, the Sword of Allah.
seo.keywords: Khalid ibn al-Walid, Islamic History, Military Strategy, Sword of Allah
